elements.title: a severed hairy leg
elements.contents: ... that a severed hairy leg (pictured) signalled censorship in a newspaper during the Brazilian dictatorship?

elements.title: Andrew Mroczek
elements.contents: ... that artist Andrew Mroczek curated an exhibition treating T-shirts as fine art objects?

elements.title: Lebanese wild apple
elements.contents: ... that the European population of the rare Lebanese wild apple is mainly concentrated in a single national park, 58 percent of which was burnt by extreme wildfires in 2023?

elements.title: Nala Ray
elements.contents: ... that Nala Ray, the daughter of a Baptist minister, left an OnlyFans channel making millions of dollars per year to return to Christianity?

elements.title: Forum Lingkar Pena
elements.contents: ... that the writers' collective Forum Lingkar Pena has been called a "factory for story writers"?

elements.title: Xiao
elements.contents: ... that parents once mistook a student's doodle of the video game character Xiao for a Satanic hate symbol?

elements.title: Ridgeway Plaza
elements.contents: ... that Ridgeway Plaza calls itself North America's largest halal food market?

elements.title: Cameron Fraser-Monroe
elements.contents: ... that ballet choreographer Cameron Fraser-Monroe applied to the Royal Winnipeg Ballet School on a whim and received a scholarship?

elements.title: turned Wikipedia into a card-collecting gacha game
elements.contents: ... that a Japanese developer turned Wikipedia into a card-collecting gacha game?
